\text{Step 1: Dive in with the Law of Sines to find angle } B.

\frac{\sin(B)}{b} = \frac{\sin(A)}{a}

\sin(B) = \frac{12.4\sin(30^\circ)}{2.9} \approx 2.1379

\text{Step 2: Attempt to find the angle using inverse sine.}

B = \sin^{-1}(2.1379)

\text{Wait! The domain of the inverse sine function is } [-1, 1]\text{, but } 2.1379 > 1.

\text{If you type this into a calculator, you will get a domain error. Geometrically, this tells us the swinging side } a \text{ is too short to reach the base.}

\text{Therefore, there are exactly ZERO valid triangles.}
